div1.entryFree: Under, adv. below: sealed u. for another, Merch. I, 2, 89. to bring u. == to bring down, to bring low: the least of all these maladies . . . brings beauty u. Ven. 746. Similarly: the wars have so kept you u. All's I, 1, 209, an expression used by way of quibbling, like Launce's grammatical observation: stand u. and understand is all one, Gent. II, 5, 33.
